title: Cephaelis appendiculata
intro: Cephaelis appendiculata, commonly known as the "Ipecacuanha plant," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its emetic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing health benefits such as relieving nausea and reducing fever. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis appendiculata include emetine and cephaeline, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into cough syrups and expectorants due to its ability to stimulate respiratory secretions.
taxonomy: Cephaelis appendiculata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Within the Rubiaceae family, Cephaelis appendiculata is placed in the genus Cephaelis, which comprises various species of plants often referred to as "hot cocoa trees." The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the madder family. Cephaelis appendiculata's classification is further refined by its botanical name, which includes its genus, species, and any additional descriptive information.

parts: Cephaelis appendiculata, also known as the yellow-flowered Cephaelis, is a plant species used in traditional medicine. preparations: Cephaelis appendiculata is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, where it's believed to have various health benefits.
